statistics.name: assists
statistics.description: The number of times a player who passes the ball to a teammate in a way that leads to a score by field goal, meaning that he or she was "assisting" in the basket. There is some judgment involved in deciding whether a passer should be credited with an assist.

articles.headline: Indiana puts home win streak on the line against Atlanta
articles.description: Atlanta Dream (9-4, 4-2 Eastern Conference) at Indiana Fever (9-5, 5-2 Eastern Conference)

articles.headline: Brittney Sykes exits Tempo's loss to Fever with foot injury
articles.description: The Tempo's Brittney Sykes left the court in the third quarter with a noncontact foot injury. Toronto coach Sandy Brondello said Sykes will be evaluated and the hope was the injury isn't too serious.

articles.headline: Mitchell scores 27, Clark has 21 points and 14 assists, and the Fever beat the Tempo 113-91
articles.description: — Kelsey Mitchell scored 27 points on 9-of-11 shooting, Caitlin Clark and Aliyah Boston each had a double-double, and the Indiana Fever beat the Toronto Tempo 113-91 on Tuesday night for their fourth straight victory.

articles.headline: Gabby Williams scores 16 points, Kaila Charles comes off bench for 13, Valkyries top Sparks 78-58
articles.description: — Gabby Williams scored 16 points, Kaila Charles came of the bench to finish with 13 points, eight rebounds and five assists, and the Golden State Valkyries beat the Los Angeles Sparks 78-58 on Monday night for their third straight win.

articles.headline: Arike Ogunbowale scores 22 points and hits 5 3-pointers in the Wings' 96-66 victory over the Aces
articles.description: — Arike Ogunbowale scored 22 points and made five 3-pointers, Jessica Shepard had 15 points, 15 rebounds and nine assists, and the Dallas Wings beat the Las Vegas Aces 96-66 on Monday night for their fifth straight win at home.

article.headline: Wilson's 28 points and 15 rebounds, Young's 23 points and 9 assists power Aces past Valkyries
article.description: — A'ja Wilson had 28 points, 15 rebounds and four blocks, Jackie Young added 23 points and nine assists, and the Las Vegas Aces beat the Golden State Valkyries 91-81 on Sunday.
article.linkText: Wilson's 28 points and 15 rebounds, Young's 23 points and 9 assists power Aces past Valkyries

article.story: SAN FRANCISCO -- — A'ja Wilson had 28 points, 15 rebounds and four blocks, Jackie Young added 23 points and nine assists, and the Las Vegas Aces beat the Golden State Valkyries 91-81 on Sunday. The victory was the 150th of Becky Hammon's coaching career, and it made her the second-fastest coach to reach the milestone. Hammon did it in 205 games — seven behind Van Chancellor's record set when he coached the Houston Comets. Young made 9 of 17 from the field, 5 of 9 from 3-point range, and grabbed seven rebounds. NaLyssa Smith had 15 points and nine rebounds for the Aces (5-3), the defending WNBA champions. Gabby Williams led the Valkyries (5-3) with 20 points, Janelle Salaun added 16 and Kayla Thornton 12. Las Vegas led 43-41 at halftime before Wilson had 13 points and Young 11 in the third quarter as the Aces outscored Golden State 29-13 to take an 18-point lead into the fourth. The Valkyries made just 3 of 17 (18%) from the field and committed five turnovers in the period. Williams hit a 3-pointer that capped and 11-0 run and trimmed Golden State's deficit to 85-74 with 1:52 left in the game. Salaun made two free throws and then hit a 3 that made it an eight-point game about a minute later, but the Valkyries never got closer. The Valkyries, who went into the game allowing a WNBA-low 78 points per game, gave up a season-high 91 points. Golden State's Veronica Burton went to the bench with about 3 1/2 minutes remaining and was in obvious discomfort as she grabbed at her left thigh. The team’s leading scorer at 15.9 per game, Burton had seven points and five assists. There was no immediate word on the extent of her injury. Chennedy Carter took a couple of hard falls in the first half, walked slowly to locker room at halftime and played just one minute in the third quarter before leaving the game. Carter finished with six points in nine minutes. Up next Aces: Visit Los Angeles on Tuesday. Valkyries: Host Portland on Tuesday. ------ AP WNBA: https://apnews.com/hub/wnba-basketball
